# Directory: tests/fixtures/ab-corpus-2/

## Purpose
Self-contained, sealed corpus + tooling + raw results of pre-registered experiment 078 — the
tier-diverse validation of R3c (does model-tier heterogeneity earn the committee's escalation?).
Verdicts in `docs/reviews/tier-diverse-experiment-078.md`; human methodology in local `README.md`
(EN) / `README.ru.md` (RU). Sibling: `../ab-corpus/` (experiment 075).
**Sealed:** any edit to `files/*` or `ground_truth.json` invalidates `seal.json` — re-run
`build_ground_truth.py` (new seal) and discard ALL prior runs in `results/`.

## Python modules

### files/ — review corpus (7 standalone modules, data-pipeline theme, no imports between them)
Seeded files carry exactly 3 bugs each (1 logic + 1 security + 1 performance; lines/severities in `ground_truth.json`):

- `g1_csv_ingest.py` — CSV parse/coerce/export. Seeded: parse() builds a header index map but reads by hardcoded positions row[0..3] → silent field corruption on reordered columns (L/CRIT); CSV formula injection on export, no =,+,-,@ neutralization (S/HIGH); count_rows readlines() loads whole file (P/MED).
- `g2_rate_limiter.py` — sliding-window limiter. Seeded: `<=` allows limit+1 requests/window (L/HIGH); identity trusts client-controlled X-Forwarded-For → trivial bypass (S/CRIT); list.pop(0) O(n) eviction, should be deque (P/MED).
- `g3_email_queue.py` — outbound email. Seeded: dedup keyed only by recipient drops distinct messages (L/MED); CRLF header injection via raw f-string into sendmail (S/CRIT); new SMTP connection per message in flush loop (P/HIGH).
- `g4_inventory.py` — stock/reservations. Seeded: reserve uses `>` not `>=`, can't reserve exact remaining stock (L/HIGH); SQL injection via f-string in admin name search (S/MED); N+1 movements query per product in low_stock_report (P/CRIT).
- `g5_graph_traversal.py` — dependency graph. Seeded: reachable() enqueues neighbors with no visited guard → infinite loop on cycles (L/CRIT); log injection via unescaped node id (S/MED); count_paths recurses without memo, exponential (P/HIGH).
- `g6_session_store.py` — sessions + TTL. Seeded: touch() slides expiry without checking current → resurrects expired session (L/MED); tokens via random not secrets, predictable (S/HIGH); _purge_expired full O(n) sweep on every get() (P/CRIT).
- `ctrl_aggregate.py` — clean control (numeric aggregation helpers; intentionally bug-free).

### Tooling
- `build_ground_truth.py` — one-shot generator: locates each seeded bug by unique anchor substring, derives line numbers (never hand-counted), asserts stratification (6/6/6 by class AND by severity), writes `ground_truth.json` + sha256 `seal.json`. Re-running re-seals.
- `analyze.py` — frozen-pre-data scorer. `match_bug()` = same file + |Δline|≤3 + class; `load_committee()` = deterministic Phase-2 merge of a critic trio; `score_arm()` adds **overlap precision** (fraction of 2-critic same-location/class overlaps that hit a real bug — the T3 metric); `main()` evaluates rules T1/T2/T3. Run: `python3 analyze.py`.

## Data artifacts
- `ground_truth.json` — sealed key: 18 bugs × {id, file, line, class, severity, description} + control + stratification counts.
- `seal.json` — sha256 of all 7 corpus files + ground_truth.json, UTC seal timestamp (2026-06-10T12:53:58Z — predates first run).
- `scan_floor.json` / `scan_summary.txt` — raw `run_audit.py` output (≈ empty here — corpus-2 bugs are semantic, not regex-detectable; this is intentional, it de-saturates the security class).
- `analysis.json` — full scorer output: per-arm per-run metrics, overlap precision, T1/T2/T3 verdicts.
- `results/A/<file>__r<k>.json` — 21 single-reviewer reports `{arm, file, run, findings[], convergence}`.
- `results/Dsame/<file>__r<k>/{logic,security,performance}.json` — 63 same-model (opus×3) critic reports.
- `results/Dtier/<file>__r<k>/{logic,security,performance}.json` — 63 tier-diverse (sonnet/opus/fable) critic reports.
- `results/wallclock.log` — start/done stamps, token totals and durations per arm.
- `results/token_split.json` — per-arm input/output/cache split, aggregated from agent-transcript usage blocks.

## Invariants
1. Seal-before-run: `seal.json` timestamp must predate every result file.
2. Reviewer agents never see `ground_truth.json`, `seal.json`, `.AGENTS.md`, or the READMEs — only one file from `files/`.
3. `analyze.py` semantics (including the overlap-precision T3 metric) are part of the pre-registration — do not "improve" them post-hoc; a new scoring idea = a new experiment.
4. D-same vs D-tier differ ONLY in the per-critic model assignment (opus×3 vs sonnet/opus/fable) — the prompts, schema, and merge are identical, so any metric delta is the model axis.
5. Cross-seal comparisons are invalid (different corpus hash ⇒ different experiment). This corpus is also distinct from `../ab-corpus/` (different domain, different bug count).
